mappings.rationaleAI: The Industrial Revolution covers textile manufacturing innovations and iron/steel production, directly relevant to the textile and steel industries as major fabrication industries.

Edison's inventions and Menlo Park as the first industrial research facility connect to the electronics industry and industrial research and development.
Tesla's development of AC electrical systems and the Niagara Falls hydroelectric project relate to the electronics industry and the utilities industries.
The Stephensons' railway engineering including locomotive and bridge construction relates to the steel industry and major manufacturing.
William Morris's Arts and Crafts movement opposed industrial mass production in textiles and wallpaper, directly relevant to the textile industry and the paper industry.
Carbon's industrial applications in steel manufacturing and synthetic materials connect to the steel industry and the chemical industry.
Penicillin's wartime scaling up of production represents a landmark in the pharmaceuticals industry.
The invention of radio covers the development of wireless telegraphy and broadcasting equipment, relevant to the electronics industry.
The electron's applications from vacuum tubes to semiconductors and modern electronics directly relate to the electronics industry.
The Lunar Society members including Boulton, Watt, and Wedgwood drove the Industrial Revolution's industrial innovation in steam power, manufacturing, and ceramics.
